content:
First, note that if \(\deg f=d\ge1\) with leading term \(a x^d\), then as \(x\to\infty\)

- LHS \(=f(x-1)+f(x)+f(x+1)\sim3a\,x^d\).
- RHS \(\displaystyle=\frac{f(x)^2}{2013\,x}\sim\frac{(a x^d)^2}{2013\,x}=\frac{a^2}{2013}\,x^{2d-1}.\)

For these to have the same degree we need 
\[
d\;=\;2d-1
\quad\Longrightarrow\quad d=1.
\]
Hence \(f\) is linear, say \(f(x)=Ax+B\) with \(A\neq0\).  Then

LHS = \(f(x-1)+f(x)+f(x+1) =A(x-1)+B + Ax+B +A(x+1)+B=3Ax+3B.\)

RHS = \(\dfrac{(Ax+B)^2}{2013x}
=\frac{A^2x^2+2ABx+B^2}{2013x}
=\frac{A^2}{2013}x+\frac{2AB}{2013}+\frac{B^2}{2013x}.\)

Equating,
\[
3Ax+3B
=\frac{A^2}{2013}x+\frac{2AB}{2013}+\frac{B^2}{2013x},
\]
and since the LHS has no \(1/x\)‐term we must have \(B^2=0\), i.e.\ \(B=0\).  Then
\[
3Ax \;=\;\frac{A^2}{2013}\,x
\quad\Longrightarrow\quad
3A=\frac{A^2}{2013}
\quad\Longrightarrow\quad
A=0\text{ or }A=3\cdot2013=6039.
\]
The solution \(A=0\) gives the zero polynomial (constant), which we discard.  Thus the unique nonconstant solution is
\[
f(x)=6039\,x,
\]
and so
\[
f(1)=6039.
\]
Hence the sum of all possible values of \(f(1)\) is \(\boxed{6039}.\)

\[
d\;=\;2d-1
\quad\Longrightarrow\quad d=1.
\]
Hence \(f\) is linear, say \(f(x)=Ax+B\) with \(A\neq0\).  Then

LHS = \(f(x-1)+f(x)+f(x+1) =A(x-1)+B + Ax+B +A(x+1)+B=3Ax+3B.\)

RHS = \(\dfrac{(Ax+B)^2}{2013x}
=\frac{A^2x^2+2ABx+B^2}{2013x}
=\frac{A^2}{2013}x+\frac{2AB}{2013}+\frac{B^2}{2013x}.\)

\[
3Ax+3B

A=0\text{ or }A=3\cdot2013=6039.

\[
f(x)=6039\,x,

\[
